title: Sovereign Grace in Salvation
verseText: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out. — John 6:37
harmonies: John 6:44 — No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him.
Romans 8:30 — Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified.
Ephesians 1:4-5 — According as he hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world.
context: John 6:35-44 — Jesus declares Himself the bread of life. He states that all given by the Father will come, and that none will be cast out. He grounds belief in the sovereign giving and drawing of the Father, not in human initiation.
insights: The Father's giving and the Son's keeping are two golden links of the same unbreakable chain. Election is not cold doctrine but the warm foundation of assurance. None given will be lost.
application: Rest thy soul upon the certainty of divine election and Christ's unfailing promise: 'I will in no wise cast out.' When doubt assails, look to the Father's gift and the Son's promise, not to thine own strength.

Title: Sovereign Grace in Salvation
• The Father gives — the Son keeps
• None given shall be lost
• Rest on His promise, not thy effort
